payload.answer: I can’t durably store "everything about AI" as a memory item because it is overly broad, non-specific, and not a stable personal fact or preference. A better memory request would be a narrow, durable item such as a preference, goal, project context, or a specific fact you want retained.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific personal entity or stable fact was provided to resolve.

payload.intent: Request to store a broad, open-ended body of knowledge about AI in durable memory.

details: Rejected because the request is overly broad and effectively unbounded.
Rejected because it does not specify a durable, user-specific memory candidate.

repair_options: Ask to remember a specific preference about AI topics, such as interests or expertise level.
Ask to remember a concrete project involving AI, including tools, goals, or constraints.
Ask to remember a concise fact, e.g. "I’m interested in reinforcement learning" or "I’m building an AI chatbot."
Provide a bounded note instead of an unlimited domain-wide request.
